_comment: Entries here represent findings that have been triaged and explicitly
accepted by a reviewer. Every entry MUST include the fields documented
below. Entries that intersect a protected-module path require an
lead who acknowledged the risk, and a 14-day max `expires_at`.
Adding an entry that masks a finding in a protected path is rejected
tool:           bandit | semgrep | gitleaks | pip-audit | safety | trufflehog
finding_id:     the tool's stable identifier (e.g. B602, owasp-top-ten.sql-injection)
path:           file:line where the finding fires
reason:         why this is accepted (be specific; 'false positive' alone is rejected)
approver:       GitHub login of the reviewer who accepted this
added_at:       ISO8601 date this entry was created
expires_at:     ISO8601 date after which CI forces re-review
protected_module_acknowledged?: optional, required when path is protected
Entries past `expires_at` cause CI to fail until the entry is renewed
or the finding is fixed. There is no `expires_at = never`.
